summary: Culinary Atelier is a MuranoOS boundary-arbitration packet for food knowledge, domestic logistics, culture, and service.
final_use_case: It matters as a MuranoOS design example: even non-academic domains can be organized into sources, constraints, workflows, review, and execution.

instruments.input: user roles, task sequence, room state, and domain objects
instruments.operation: It separates Federated Studio, Service, Domestic Logistics, Culture, recipe knowledge, ingredient state, intent, red-team review, and executable meal planning so adjacent private pages are consolidated into one professional operating room without exposing private content.

instruments.instrument_name: Culinary Atelier Data Schema And Persistence Plan
instruments.input: entities, records, IDs, tables, JSON structures, indexes, and state transitions
instruments.operation: Discovery, creativity, systems thinking, and project generation. Use source library creativity/innovation/network sources to professionalize the project-development account. Vision: Explain Nexus and Personal OS as project-development and discovery infrastructure, not miscellaneous personal tooling. Mechanism: Extract creativity, network, learning, imagination, leadership, and project-generation mechanisms. Plan: Rewrite Nexus and planning pages around upstream/downstream project movement. Status: source library.

instruments.input: frontend, backend, storage, retrieval, auth, export, and portfolio linkage
instruments.operation: How this briefing says the vision should be accomplished, translated into a public project route. Reach for this when: (a) you need a reference boundary-arbitration on how to consolidate multiple routes into one governed room without breaking the existing URLs (proposes canonical + deep-link resolution); (b) you want an 8-engine culinary workflow pattern with well-defined inputs/outputs; (c) you are consolidating any domain (health, finance, writing) with multiple overlapping pages into one governed MuranoOS room.

instruments.input: project source text and the named component `Federated Studio`
instruments.operation: treats `Federated Studio` as a room or workbench component that gives the project a usable surface rather than only a written description. Source context: The package proposes one canonical room, CulinaryAtelierRoom (display name "Atelier", archetype "Federated Studio"), that unifies study, design, execution, and review of food work. Canonical route: /food-lab. /health/food-science becomes a deep link to the Study scene; /health/cooking becomes a deep link to Compose or Service. Primary domain proposed as Health; cross-domain integrations proposed for Learning.
